news.title: STAT+: FDA drug center head Tracy Beth Høeg leaves as agency faces leadership vacuum
news.summary: The acting director of the FDA’s drug center, Tracy Beth Høeg, is leaving the agency. The news come days after Commissioner Marty Makary handed in his resignation.

news.title: STAT+: Takeda will pay $13.6 million to settle allegations it paid kickbacks to doctors
news.summary: Takeda Pharmaceuticals agreed to pay $13.6 million to settle allegations that it paid kickbacks to doctors to prescribe an antidepressant.

news.title: STAT+: Up and down the ladder: The latest comings and goings
news.summary: From new hires to departures, promotions and transfers, here are the latest comings and goings in the pharmaceutical industry.

news.title: Opinion: First hantavirus, now Ebola: What two outbreaks reveal about global preparedness
news.summary: “Preparedness is not built during a crisis press conference or after a pathogen has already crossed borders,” writes Krutika Kuppalli.

news.title: A new Ebola outbreak is confirmed in a remote Congo province, with 65 deaths recorded
news.summary: A new Ebola outbreak has been confirmed in Congo’s remote Ituri province, with 246 suspected cases and 65 deaths recorded so far.

news.title: STAT+: A political contest in the U.K. could shake up drug pricing
news.summary: Makary and Cassidy's fates, U.S. pressures Germany to pay more for drugs, and other health news

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about U.S. pressuring Germany on drug prices, FDA upheaval and biotech clinical trials, and more
news.summary: In today's Pharmalittle roundup: U.S. pressuring Germany on drug prices, FDA upheaval and biotech clinical trials, and much more.

news.title: Opinion: Telehealth abortion with mifepristone continues, with backup plans still in place
news.summary: Restricting telehealth prescribing of mifepristone wouldn't spell the end of telehealth medication abortion, writes OB-GYN Christine Henneberg.

news.title: Mail-order abortion pills are legal (for now)
news.summary: Africa CDC declares Ebola outbreak in DRC, the violent fallout from Trump’s cuts to foreign aid, and more health news

news.title: DOJ accuses Yale and UCLA medical schools of discriminating against white and Asian applicants
news.summary: The Justice Department sent a letter to Yale School of Medicine on Thursday, alleging it was illegally discriminating against applicants who are not Black or Hispanic.

news.title: STAT+: Top U.S. officials pressured Germany to pay more for prescription drugs
news.summary: It's a key piece of Trump's effort to secure lower drug prices for U.S. patients.

news.title: Opinion: Tributes to Craig Venter and the genomics race are missing something important
news.summary: A race, two rivals, a photo finish? Former National Human Genome Research Institute archivist explains what Craig Venter tributes are missing.

news.title: Why isn’t alcohol seen as a public health emergency?
news.summary: Why isn't alcohol seen as a public health emergency? Alex Hogan talks to STAT's Isabella Cueto and Lev Facher about their new series on this week's STATus Report.

news.title: Opinion: I’m 73. I wish my pills wouldn’t keep changing colors and shapes
news.summary: Pharmaceutical companies, generic manufacturers, and pharmacists should be more careful about changing how pills look.

news.title: PSA screening for prostate cancer reduces disease-specific deaths, new review shows
news.summary: Prostate-specific antigen (PSA) blood testing likely reduces the risk of death from prostate cancer, found a new review

news.title: Texas Children's Hospital agrees to launch nation's first 'detransition clinic' to settle state, DOJ investigation
news.summary: The large pediatric provider had been under investigation by Texas' attorney general for three years over its gender-affirming care services. It said it agreed to the deal "to protect our resources from endless and costly litigation."

news.title: Kinderhook finalizes acquisition of home health and hospice provider Enhabit
news.summary: Kinderhook Industries finalized its previously announced take-private buyout of Enhabit, a provider of home health and hospice care.

news.title: Docs remain skeptical of insurers' pledge to ease prior auth: AMA survey
news.summary: As payers tout progress made under a pledge to ease burdens around prior authorizations, physicians remain skeptical, a new survey shows.

news.title: Op-ed: Making America healthy again requires more than a committee
news.summary: The question for the administration's soon-to-convene Federal Advisory Committee to improve U.S. healthcare is not whether we know what to do, but whether we are finally willing to do it, write the CEOs of the American Academy of Family Physicians and SCAN Group.

news.title: Epic grows EHR footprint among small health systems even as overall market sales decline in 2025
news.summary: The number of hospitals impacted by EHR purchase decisions dropped 40% compared to 2024, KLAS Research reported. Despite the slowdown, Epic's share of the U.S. EHR market continued to inch up, primarily driven by contract decisions at smaller health systems.

news.title: SCOTUS temporarily restores telehealth access to abortion pill mifepristone
news.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court has issued an emergency order, temporarily blocking a federal appeals court ruling in Louisiana which would have prevented the abortion pill mifepristone from being prescribed over the phone and sent through the mail in the United States.

news.title: Washington AG alleges Providence failed to accommodate pregnant, nursing employees
news.summary: A lawsuit brought this week by the state alleges the nonprofit system "regularly" refused, delayed or failed to implement accommodations for pregnant and nursing employees as required by law, and that some of those employees "experienced retaliation" following their requests.

news.title: Health Tech Weekly Rundown: Headspace app launches on Apple Watch; Visby, RAINN partner on safe access program
news.summary: Stay up to date on the latest in health tech, digital health and health AI news with this weekly brief.

news.title: Chutes & Ladders—Alignment Healthcare shuffles leadership team; Highmark hires former Optum CEO
news.summary: Welcome to this week's Chutes & Ladders, our roundup of hirings, firings and retirings throughout the industry. Please submit the good news—or the bad—from your shop, and we will feature it here at the end of each week.

news.title: Epic, Oracle and Cleveland Clinic among companies to joining CMS prior authorization initiative
news.summary: A slew of additional "early adopters" from different corners of the industry said they will tackle the workflow, technical and operational barriers that have stymied adoption of electronic prior authorization, CMS said this week.

news.title: Bipartisan lawmakers reintroduce bill barring PBMs from owning pharmacies
news.summary: A bipartisan group of lawmakers in both chambers of Congress has reintroduced a bill aimed at barring companies from owning both a pharmacy benefit manager and retail pharmacies.

news.title: Fierce Healthcare Fundraising Tracker '26: 9amHealth picks up $26M; Anomaly nabs $17M for payer intelligence platform
news.summary: Keep up with all the venture capital dollars being funneled into the health tech and digital health industries.

news.title: Using Digital Technologies to Enhance Patient Experience and Better Manage Supply Chain
news.summary: Integrating connectivity into drug delivery devices as a way to improve patient engagement and adherence

news.title: Ensuring Sustainability Throughout Drug and Device Development
news.summary: How the biopharma and drug delivery industries actively move to embed sustainability into core principles, products and processes

news.title: Utilizing AI to Facilitate Combination Product Development
news.summary: Specific examples of AI/ML usage by pharma companies through the combination product development process

news.title: Achieving Effective Manufacturing Scaleup for Clinical and Commercial Success
news.summary: Effective partnering between pharma and manufacturing as well as growing pains and lessons learned in scaling

news.title: Advances and Opportunities in Dual Chamber Injectables
news.summary: In this panel discussion from the 2025 PODD: Partnership Opportunities in Drug Delivery Conference, pharma executives and drug delivery experts discuss progess in the development of liquid/liquid and liquid/lyo dual chamber systems.

news.title: What a Strategic Partnership Looks Like in Practice: Inside the Takeda–Basil Collaboration That Delivered Tenfold Enrollment
news.summary: This case study highlights how Basil Clinical Research and Takeda’s first-time partnership became the top global recruiter, exceeding enrollment goals by tenfold through proactive assessments, early collaboration, and inclusive design.

news.title: Eli Lilly on Site Enablement: Co-Designing Solutions with Sites
news.summary: A case study highlighting how Eli Lilly partnered with clinical sites to co-design practical, site-centric solutions that enhanced trial execution and operational efficiency.

news.title: Building Trust-Driven Partnerships: AstraZeneca’s Concierge Approach to Accelerating Site Activation and Enrollment
news.summary: AstraZeneca’s approach to collaboration has enabled faster site activation, stronger recruitment performance, and improved data delivery.

news.title: Bridging Access Gaps: Sponsor-Led Enablement of Research-Naïve Sites to Expand Patient Reach
news.summary: This case study shares how Endo enabled a new site through hands-on mentorship, infrastructure support, and trust-building.

news.title: UPDATED: After Makary departure, CDER chief Tracy Beth Høeg headed out at FDA
news.summary: With the ink barely dry on outgoing commissioner Marty Makary’s resignation, another top regulator is heading for the exit at the FDA.

news.title: Novartis, amid reorg, cuts ‘select number’ of biomedical research roles
news.summary: After a series of staff consolidation moves in recent weeks, Novartis is again trimming its ranks, albeit in what appears to be a more limited move centered on the Swiss drugmaker’s biomedical research arm.

news.title: Why Model Medicines needs to ‘discover new colors’ in its AI drug discovery quest
news.summary: “Outliers are what you're going for,” Model Medicines CEO Daniel Haders, Ph.D., told Fierce to explain the company's AI-driven drug development strategy.

news.title: 阿里健康做对了一半
news.summary: 三年等得起吗？

papers.title: Darwin Family: MRI-Trust-Weighted Evolutionary Merging for Training-Free Scaling of Language-Model Reasoning
papers.summary: We present Darwin Family, a framework for training-free evolutionary merging of large language models via gradient-free weight-space recombination. We ask whether frontier-level reasoning performance can be improved without additional training, by reorganizing latent capabilities already encoded in existing checkpoints. Darwin introduces three key ideas: (i) a 14-dimensional adaptive merge genome enabling fine-grained component- and block-level recombination; (ii) MRI-Trust Fusion, which adaptively balances diagnostic layer-importance signals with evolutionary search through a learnable trust

papers.title: EvolveMem:Self-Evolving Memory Architecture via AutoResearch for LLM Agents
papers.summary: Long-term memory is essential for LLM agents that operate across multiple sessions, yet existing memory systems treat retrieval infrastructure as fixed: stored content evolves while scoring functions, fusion strategies, and answer-generation policies remain frozen at deployment. We argue that truly adaptive memory requires co-evolution at two levels: the stored knowledge and the retrieval mechanism that queries it. We present EvolveMem, a self-evolving memory architecture that exposes its full retrieval configuration as a structured action space optimized by an LLM-powered diagnosis module. In

papers.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
papers.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has emerged as a central paradigm for post-training LLM agents, yet its trajectory-level reward signal provides only coarse supervision for long-horizon interaction. On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) complements RL by introducing dense token-level guidance from a teacher branch augmented with privileged context. However, transferring OPSD to multi-turn agents proves problematic: compounding multi-turn instability destabilizes supervision, while skill-conditioned privileged guidance requires asymmetric treatment for negative teacher rejections may arise from imperfe

papers.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
papers.summary: Generative video models are increasingly studied as implicit world models, yet evaluating whether they produce physically plausible 3D structure and motion remains challenging. Most existing video evaluation pipelines rely heavily on human judgment or learned graders, which can be subjective and weakly diagnostic for geometric failures. We introduce PDI-Bench (Perspective Distortion Index), a quantitative framework for auditing geometric coherence in generated videos. Given a generated clip, we obtain object-centric observations via segmentation and point tracking (e.g., SAM 2, MegaSaM, and Co

papers.title: Beyond Individual Intelligence: Surveying Collaboration, Failure Attribution, and Self-Evolution in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
papers.summary: LLM-based autonomous agents have demonstrated strong capabilities in reasoning, planning, and tool use, yet remain limited when tasks require sustained coordination across roles, tools, and environments. Multi-agent systems address this through structured collaboration among specialized agents, but tighter coordination also amplifies a less explored risk: errors can propagate across agents and interaction rounds, producing failures that are difficult to diagnose and rarely translate into structural self-improvement. Existing surveys cover individual agent capabilities, multi-agent collaboratio
